title: Fix Radio Button CSS Styling on Login Page
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the radio buttons on the login page to be properly styled and visually correct, so that the first impression of the app looks polished.
description:
The radio button control on the login page has a CSS styling issue that makes it appear slightly off. Since the login page is the first thing users see, visual polish is important.

## Root Cause
CSS centralization effort (moving per-page CSS/JS into a shared location) introduced minor styling regressions on some controls, including the login page radio buttons.
acceptance_criteria: Radio buttons on the login page display with correct CSS styling
Radio button alignment and sizing match the design standard
No visual regression on other login page elements
ux_changes: Fixed CSS styling on login page radio buttons to restore correct visual appearance after CSS centralization changes.
implementation_notes: CSS regression caused by earlier centralization of per-page styles into shared stylesheet. Fixed by adjusting the radio button styles in the centralized CSS.
comment_summary: Developer fixed the radio button styling and noted the issue was a side effect of centralizing per-page CSS/JS into a shared location a few weeks prior. Minor regressions may still surface.
